Last login: Thu Mar 5 16:46:18 on ttys021 direnv: loading ~/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-03/.envrc direnv: loading ~/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-03/.erk/bin/activate.sh -> cd /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-03 -> Activating venv: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-03/.venv (3.13.1) -> Loading .env Activated: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-03 OK workspace:25 direnv: export +REPO_ROOT +VIRTUAL_ENV +VIRTUAL_ENV_PROMPT +WORKTREE_NAME +WORKTREE_PATH ~PATH ➜ erk-slot-03 git:(plnd/fix-incremental-dispatch-s-03-05-1642) erk-activate direnv: loading ~/code/erk/.envrc direnv: loading ~/code/erk/.erk/bin/activate.sh -> cd /Users/schrockn/code/erk -> Activating venv: /Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv (3.13.1) Activated: /Users/schrockn/code/erk OK workspace:25 direnv: export +VIRTUAL_ENV +VIRTUAL_ENV_PROMPT ~PATH -> cd /Users/schrockn/code/erk -> Activating venv: /Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv (3.13.1) Activated: /Users/schrockn/code/erk (erk) ➜ erk git:(master) git log (erk) ➜ erk git:(master) claude

❯ 2e0bb588e7d878057c7b73a5924dae86ac25b4bb
this had additional suggested follow. what was it. look at the pr
⏺ Bash(gh pr list --search "2e0bb588e" --state merged --json number,title --limit 5 2>/dev/null || gh log --oneline 2e0bb588e 2>/dev/null; git log --oneline 2e0bb588e -1) ⎿ [ { "number": 8786, "title": "Optimize PR submit pipeline by caching Graphite auth checks and eliminating redundant API calls" } ] 2e0bb588e Optimize PR submit pipeline by caching Graphite auth checks and eliminating redundant API calls (#8786)
⏺ Bash(gh pr view 8786 --json body --jq '.body') ⎿ ## Summary
This change reduces unnecessary GitHub and Graphite API calls in the PR submit pipeline by caching authentication and branch tracking status during the prepare_state step, and constructing the PR URL from git remote metadata instead of making additional API requests.
## Key Changes
- Cache Graphite authentication and branch tracking status in SubmitState during prepare_state to avoid redundant checks in push_and_create_pr and enhance_with_graphite functions - Replace should_enhance_with_graphite() call with direct cached boolean checks in push_and_create_pr - Eliminate GitHub API call for PR URL construction in _core_submit_flow by parsing git remote URL instead - Reuse pre-captured existing_pr_body from capture_existing_pr_body step instead of making another get_pr call - Remove redundant GitHub and Graphite API calls from finalize_pr function
<details> <summary>Files Changed</summary>
### Modified (13 files) - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` - Caching strategy, API call elimination, state field additions - `tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/test_*.py` - Updated state initialization with new cached fields
</details>
**Remotely executed:** [Run #22742773899](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22742773899)
---
To replicate this PR locally, run:
``` erk pr teleport 8786 ```
<details> <summary>original-plan</summary>
# Improve `erk pr submit` Performance
## Context
`erk pr submit` runs an 11-step linear pipeline that makes many redundant network calls and runs independent steps sequentially. The pipeline accumulates GitHub API calls, Graphite auth checks, and branch lookups that duplicate work already done in earlier steps. This plan addresses the low-hanging fruit for performance improvement.
## Key Files
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` — Main pipeline (all steps) - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_cmd.py` — CLI entry point + PR cache polling - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/pr/graphite_enhance.py` — Graphite check
## Problem Analysis
### 1. Redundant GitHub API Calls (biggest win)
The pipeline makes up to **7 separate GitHub API calls** for PR info, many redundant:
| Line | Call | Purpose | Redundant? | |------|------|---------|------------| | 229 | `get_pr_for_branch` | Capture existing PR body | Needed | | 332 | `get_pr_for_branch` | Get PR after gt submit | Needed | | 458 | `get_pr_for_branch` | Check existing PR (core flow) | Needed | | 495 | `get_pr` | Get PR URL after create | **Yes** — URL can be constructed | | 523 | `get_pr` | Check for footer (existing PR) | Needed | | 770 | `get_pr` | Check draft status | Needed | | 823 | `get_pr_for_branch` | Get "final" PR URL | **Yes** — already in state |
**Fix:** Remove line 823 (`get_pr_for_branch` at end of `finalize_pr`) — `state.pr_url` is already set by `push_and_create_pr`. Remove the `get_pr` at line 495 — construct URL from repo info instead.
### 2. Redundant Auth & Branch Checks
- `should_enhance_with_graphite` (called in `push_and_create_pr`) re-does `check_auth_status`, `get_repository_root`, `get_current_branch`, and `get_all_branches` — all already resolved by `prepare_state` - `enhance_with_graphite` (line 663) calls `check_auth_status` again - `finalize_pr` (line 829) calls `check_auth_status` a third time
**Fix:** Cache graphite auth result and branch tracking status in `SubmitState` during `prepare_state`, pass it through instead of re-checking. Or refactor `should_enhance_with_graphite` to accept pre-resolved values.
### 3. Sequential Independent Steps
`extract_diff` and `fetch_plan_context` are completely independent but run sequentially. Together they add 500-2500ms of wall time.
**Fix:** Run them concurrently using `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor`. Both are I/O-bound (git diff + GitHub API), making threading appropriate.
### 4. PR Cache Polling (up to 10s)
After submit, the pipeline polls Graphite cache for up to 10 seconds (`_wait_for_pr_in_cache`). This is purely for status line display.
**Fix:** Either reduce the timeout, make it non-blocking, or skip it when the status line isn't active. Could also run it in a background thread.
### 5. `_core_submit_flow` Redundancy
In the core flow for existing PRs (line 523), it calls `get_pr` to check the footer, even though `capture_existing_pr_body` already fetched the PR body. The existing body is in `state.existing_pr_body`.
**Fix:** Use `state.existing_pr_body` to check for footer instead of making another API call.
## Recommended Changes (This PR)
### Change 1: Eliminate Redundant API Calls in `finalize_pr`
In `finalize_pr`, remove the `get_pr_for_branch` call at the end (line 823). The `pr_url` is already set by `push_and_create_pr` and doesn't change. Just use `state.pr_url`.
Similarly, remove the `check_auth_status` + `get_graphite_url` block at lines 828-834 — `graphite_url` is already set by either `_graphite_first_flow` or `enhance_with_graphite`.
### Change 2: Use `state.existing_pr_body` for Footer Check
In `_core_submit_flow` (line 523), when checking for an existing PR's footer, use `state.existing_pr_body` from `capture_existing_pr_body` instead of calling `get_pr` again. Requires checking that the existing body was captured for this PR.
### Change 3: Cache Graphite Check Results
Add `graphite_is_authed` and `graphite_branch_tracked` fields to `SubmitState`. Populate them once during `prepare_state`. Use these cached values in `push_and_create_pr`, `enhance_with_graphite`, and `finalize_pr` instead of re-calling `check_auth_status` and `get_all_branches`.
## Follow-on Work (separate PRs)
- Parallelize `extract_diff` + `fetch_plan_context` with ThreadPoolExecutor - Reduce PR cache polling from 10s to 3s or make conditional
## Verification
1. Run `erk pr submit` on a branch with changes and verify it still creates/updates PRs correctly 2. Run `erk pr submit --skip-description` to verify the fast path 3. Run `erk pr submit --no-graphite` to verify the core flow 4. Run existing tests: `pytest tests/ -k submit` 5. Time before/after with `time erk pr submit` to measure improvement
</details>
<!-- WARNING: Machine-generated. Manual edits may break erk tooling. --> <!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-header --> <details> <summary>plan-header</summary>

⏺ The PR's plan has a Follow-on Work section with two items:
1. Parallelize extract_diff + fetch_plan_context with ThreadPoolExecutor — these two steps are independent and I/O-bound but currently run sequentially, adding 500-2500ms of wall time 2. Reduce PR cache polling from 10s to 3s or make conditional — after submit, _wait_for_pr_in_cache polls Graphite cache for up to 10 seconds purely for status line display
⏺ User answered Claude's questions: ⎿ · Would you like to plan the implementation of either follow-up item? → i want to dig in and understand 2
⏺ Let me explore the PR cache polling code.
⏺ Search(pattern: "_wait_for_pr_in_cache", path: "~/code/erk") ⎿ Found 15 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
⏺ Read(src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_cmd.py) ⎿ Read 80 lines
⏺ Read(src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_and_create_pr.py) ⎿ Read 42 lines ⎿ Loaded src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/CLAUDE.md ⎿ Loaded src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/AGENTS.md
⏺ Now let me see how the polling is called from submit_cmd.py and understand what it's for.
⏺ Read(src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_cmd.py) ⎿ Read 32 lines
⏺ Search(pattern: "get_prs_from_graphite", path: "~/code/erk") ⎿ Found 19 files (ctrl+o to expand)
⏺ Let me check what get_prs_from_graphite actually does — this is what's being called repeatedly during polling.
⏺ Search(pattern: "def get_prs_from_graphite", path: "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite") ⎿ Found 6 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
⏺ Search(pattern: "status.line|status_line|statusline", path: "src/erk/status") ⎿ Found 0 files
⏺ Read(packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/real.py) ⎿ Read 40 lines
⏺ Read(src/erk/status/collectors/github.py) ⎿ Read 76 lines
⏺ Here's what I found about the PR cache polling:
How it works
What it polls: get_prs_from_graphite reads .git/.graphite_pr_info — a local JSON file that Graphite writes after gt submit. It's a local file read, not a network call.
Why it exists: After gt submit creates/updates a PR, there's a delay before Graphite updates .graphite_pr_info. The polling ensures the PR is in the local cache so the status line collector (GitHubPRCollector at src/erk/status/collectors/github.py) can immediately display PR info (number, URL, state) without making GitHub API calls.
Two call sites: 1. submit_cmd.py:192 — interactive erk pr submit (guarded by ENABLE_PR_CACHE_POLLING flag) 2. push_and_create_pr.py:83 — exec script variant (always runs when graphite_url is set)
Config: 10s max wait, 0.5s poll interval = up to 20 file reads before timeout.
The trade-off: You wait up to 10s after submit so the status line immediately shows PR info. If it times out, the status line just won't show PR info until Graphite eventually updates the file.
⏺ User answered Claude's questions: ⎿ · What specifically do you want to understand or decide about the cache polling? → this should absolutely not be in the erk pr submit critical path. anything to help the statusline should only be in the statusline
⏺ Clear direction. Let me look at what cleanup is needed.
⏺ Read(src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_cmd.py) ⎿ Read 35 lines
⏺ Read(src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/push_and_create_pr.py) ⎿ Read 30 lines
⏺ Read(tests/commands/pr/test_submit_pr_cache_polling.py) ⎿ Read 338 lines ⎿ Loaded tests/CLAUDE.md ⎿ Loaded tests/AGENTS.md ⎿ Loaded tests/commands/CLAUDE.md ⎿ Loaded tests/commands/AGENTS.md
⏺ This is straightforward — remove the cache polling from the submit critical path entirely. The task is simple enough to skip the agent phases and go straight to the plan. ⎿ Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/melodic-weaving-grove.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Parallelize extract_diff and fetch_plan_context in PR Submit Pipeline Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#8751)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.

session.assistant:
# Parallelize extract_diff and fetch_plan_context in PR Submit Pipeline
## Context
`erk pr submit` runs an 11-step sequential pipeline. Steps 6 (`extract_diff`) and 7 (`fetch_plan_context`) are completely independent — they read disjoint parts of `SubmitState` and write disjoint fields. Running them sequentially wastes 500-2500ms of wall time. This plan parallelizes them using `ThreadPoolExecutor`.
## Key Files
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` — pipeline definition, `SubmitState`, step functions - `tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/` — unit tests for pipeline steps
## Data Flow Analysis
| Step | Reads | Writes | |------|-------|--------| | `extract_diff` (Step 6) | `skip_description`, `base_branch`, `cwd`, `debug`, `session_id`, `repo_root` | `diff_file` | | `fetch_plan_context` (Step 7) | `skip_description`, `repo_root`, `branch_name` | `plan_context` |
No overlap. Both guard on `skip_description` and return early. `generate_description` (Step 8) is the first to consume both outputs.
## Implementation Plan
### 1. Create combined `extract_diff_and_fetch_plan_context` step
Runs both steps in parallel via `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2)`. The `skip_description` guard at the top avoids spawning threads on the fast path.
Merges results by extracting the two disjoint fields each step writes and applying both to the original state.
Output interleaving fix: print a single combined `"Phase 2: Getting diff and plan context"` line before launching threads, remove the individual `Phase 2/3` echo lines from the individual functions. Debug echo lines are low-traffic and acceptable.
### 2. Update `_submit_pipeline()`
Replace the two separate step entries with the single `extract_diff_and_fetch_plan_context` step (10 steps total instead of 11).
### 3. Keep individual functions intact
`extract_diff` and `fetch_plan_context` remain as standalone functions — the combined step delegates to them. Existing tests for the individual steps are unchanged.
### 4. Add `import concurrent.futures` to `submit_pipeline.py`
### 5. Add test for the combined step
- Both fields set on success - `skip_description=True` short-circuits - `SubmitError` from either sub-step propagates
## Verification
1. `pytest tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/ -x` 2. `erk pr submit` on a branch with changes — verify PR is created and description generated 3. `time erk pr submit` before/after to confirm improvement

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': '📋 Parallelize extract_diff and fetch_plan_context in PR Submit Pipeline\nCu...[truncated 410 chars]...t the Plan', 'description': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}]}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "📋 Parallelize extract_diff and fetch_plan_context in PR Submit Pipeline Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#8751)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" --bra...[truncated 107 chars]... submit pipeline using ThreadPoolExecutor, eliminating 500-2500ms of sequential overhead."
Save plan to GitHub with branch slug and summary

Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):

session.assistant:
Implement plan #8795: In current wt: `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8795 --script)" && erk implement` (dangerously): `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8795 --script)" && erk implement -d` In new wt: `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8795 --script)" && erk implement` (dangerously): `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8795 --script)" && erk implement -d`
Checkout plan #8795: In current wt: `erk br co --for-plan 8795` In new wt: `erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8795`
Dispatch plan #8795: CLI command: `erk pr dispatch 8795` Slash command: `/erk:pr-dispatch`
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "1eb7aa42-91be-473e-84a2-08c18adcecef" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.tool_result:
Checking GitHub authentication... Syncing trunk with remote... Resolving GitHub username... Validating 1 planned-PR plan(s)...
Validating PR #8795...
✓ All 1 plan(s) validated
#8795: [erk-plan] Parallelize extract_diff and fetch_plan_context in PR Submit Pipeline
Dispatching PR #8795...
Fetching plan content... Syncing branch: plnd/parallelize-diff-fetch-03-05-1702 Committing plan to branch... ✓ Branch pushed to remote
Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!
Dispatched plans:
... [4 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Clear plan-saved marker to allow new plans in session

session.assistant:
✅ **Plan #8795 dispatched for remote implementation**
Workflow: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22744121675
The remote CI will implement the parallelization and run tests automatically. You can monitor progress via the workflow link or check the PR for updates.
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
